title: Anthropic Computer Use — cross-platform AI desktop control (API + Cowork/Dispatch)
desc: Anthropic Computer Use lets Claude drive a real desktop via screenshot + mouse/keyboard. Two paths: (1) API beta tool computer_20251124 with custom agent loop, works on any OS. (2) 2026 native integrations Claude Cowork and Claude Code directly control Mac (Mar 2026) and Windows (Apr 2026) with permission prompts. Covers tool versions, actions, agent loop, coordinate scaling, pricing, security, and comparison with OpenAI CUA and WKAppBot.
steps:
OVERVIEW AND 2026 STATUS
Computer Use is Claude ability to see a screen (screenshot) and act on it (click, type, scroll, drag). Two flavors exist in 2026:

1. API-level beta tool (since Oct 2024, still beta in Apr 2026)
   - You implement the computing environment (VM, container, native)
   - You run the agent loop, Claude just picks actions
   - Free to use beyond normal API costs; pay per token
   - Cross-platform wherever YOU implement it

2. Native integrations (2026 Q1)
   - Mar 17 2026: Dispatch (continuous thread across phone + desktop)
   - Mar 23 2026: Claude Cowork + Claude Code can directly control your Mac
   - Apr 3 2026: Windows expansion (research preview)
   - Built on Vercept acquisition (Feb 2026) UI navigation engine
   - Requires Claude Pro or Max subscription
   - Asks permission before accessing each app, sensitive apps blocked by default

TOOL VERSIONS AND BETA HEADER (as of Apr 2026)
API requires anthropic-beta header:

  computer-use-2025-11-24     latest, for Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.5
  computer-use-2025-01-24     for Sonnet 4.5 / Haiku 4.5 / Opus 4.1 / Sonnet 4 / Opus 4 / 3.7
  computer-use-2024-10-22     deprecated

Tool type strings (pick one matching your model):

  computer_20251124           zoom support, enable_zoom param
  computer_20250124           hold_key, left/middle/right_click, double_click, triple_click, scroll, wait, drag
  computer_20241022           deprecated basic set

Companion tools often used together:
  text_editor_20250728 and bash_20250124 (pair with computer_20251124)

ZDR eligible when Anthropic ZDR contract is present.

QUICK START SHAPE (curl-equivalent)
POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages
  header content-type: application/json
  header x-api-key: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
  header anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
  header anthropic-beta: computer-use-2025-11-24
  body:
    model: claude-opus-4-6
    max_tokens: 1024
    tools:
      - type: computer_20251124, name: computer, display_width_px: 1024, display_height_px: 768, display_number: 1
      - type: text_editor_20250728, name: str_replace_based_edit_tool
      - type: bash_20250124, name: bash
    messages:
      - role: user, content: Save a picture of a cat to my desktop.

Python / TypeScript / C# / Go / Java / PHP / Ruby SDKs all accept the same tool shape.

ACTIONS REFERENCE

Basic (all versions):
  screenshot                      capture display
  left_click [x, y]               click at coordinates
  type text                       type a string
  key ctrl+s                      press key combination
  mouse_move [x, y]               move cursor (no click)

Enhanced (computer_20250124 and up):
  scroll {coord, direction, amount}   scroll up/down/left/right
  left_click_drag {from, to}          drag
  right_click and middle_click        other buttons
  double_click and triple_click       multi-click
  left_mouse_down and left_mouse_up   fine-grained (spreadsheet cell select)
  hold_key {key, duration}            hold for N seconds
  wait {duration}                     pause

Modifier keys with click or scroll: add text param:
  shift / ctrl / alt / super          super is Cmd on Mac, Win key on Windows

Enhanced (computer_20251124 only, needs enable_zoom true):
  zoom {region: [x1, y1, x2, y2]}     view region at full resolution

Return format to Claude: tool_result block with screenshot or text content.

AGENT LOOP PATTERN (simplified Python)
while True and iterations less than max_iterations:
    response = client.beta.messages.create(
        model=model,
        tools=tools,
        messages=messages,
        betas=[beta_flag],
        max_tokens=max_tokens)

    messages.append({role: assistant, content: response.content})
    tool_results = []
    for block in response.content:
        if block.type == tool_use:
            result = handle_computer_action(block.name, block.input)
            tool_results.append({type: tool_result, tool_use_id: block.id, content: result})

    if not tool_results:
        break
    messages.append({role: user, content: tool_results})

Iteration cap prevents runaway API costs. Screenshot after every action is expensive batch when possible.

COORDINATE SCALING (CRITICAL GOTCHA)
API caps images at 1568px longest edge and about 1.15M total pixels.
A 1512x982 screen is downsampled to about 1330x864 before Claude sees it.
Claude returns coordinates in downsampled space, your click handler runs in real screen space.

Fix: compute scale factor once, downscale screenshots BEFORE send, upscale click coords before execute.

def get_scale_factor(w, h):
    long_edge_scale = 1568 / max(w, h)
    pixel_scale = math.sqrt(1_150_000 / (w * h))
    return min(1.0, long_edge_scale, pixel_scale)

Skipping this causes clicks to miss by offset proportional to ratio. Silent bug, easy to miss.

CROSS-PLATFORM IMPLEMENTATIONS

Linux (official): anthropic/anthropic-quickstarts computer-use-demo
  Ubuntu + Xvfb + Mutter + Tint2 inside Docker
  Web UI on port 8080, VNC on 6080
  Most battle-tested path. Start here.

macOS (community): deedy/mac_computer_use
  Native no Docker needed
  screencapture for screenshots, cliclick for mouse/keyboard
  Requires: macOS 15.7+, Python 3.12+, Homebrew, brew install cliclick
  Setup: clone, chmod +x setup.sh, pip install -r requirements, streamlit run streamlit.py
  WARNING: full unsandboxed Mac control use throwaway account or VM

Windows (community): various forks with pyautogui + pywin32
  Less mature than Mac fork
  Or use the 2026 native Claude Cowork Windows app (April preview)

Anyone: roll your own it is just HTTP + image encoding + mouse/keyboard lib.

2026 NATIVE CLAUDE INTEGRATIONS

Claude Cowork (Desktop app, GA on Mac and Windows Apr 2026)
  Browser-style conversation that can spawn a local agent to drive apps
  Built-in permission prompts per app, sensitive apps auto-blocked
  No API key management, subscription-based

Claude Code (CLI, CU enabled Mar 2026 for Mac)
  When a task needs UI (e.g. open design file, inspect website), Claude can
  click and type on your desktop instead of refusing

Dispatch (Mar 2026)
  Single conversation thread syncs between phone and desktop
  Assign task on iPhone, Claude picks up on Mac, finishes autonomously

These use an internal UI navigation stack (from Vercept acquisition) that is
more tuned than the raw API beta tool. For most end users this is the easier path.

PRICING AND TOKEN OVERHEAD (API path)

Base: standard Claude API tool-use pricing per model
Additional overhead per request using computer use:

  System prompt extension            466 to 499 tokens
  Tool definition (computer)         735 tokens (Claude 4.x)
  Tool definition (bash/text_editor) see respective docs
  Screenshots                        per Vision pricing (about 1500-2000 tokens each at 1024x768)
  Agent loop multiplier              every iteration is a full request

A 10-step task at 1024x768 can easily cost 0.05 to 0.20 USD depending on model.
Cowork and Dispatch native path: flat subscription instead.

SECURITY, LIMITATIONS, AND MITIGATIONS

Known risks:
  Prompt injection from page content or images can override user instructions
  Classifier auto-flags suspected injections and asks for confirmation
  You can opt out of classifier via Anthropic support (not recommended)
  Claude may hallucinate coordinates (mitigated by reasoning/thinking in 4.x)
  Spreadsheet cell selection still imperfect even with fine-grained mouse

Recommended mitigations:
  Run inside dedicated VM or container with minimal privileges
  No sensitive data or credentials in accessible files
  Allowlist internet domains if possible
  Human-in-the-loop confirmation for transactions, cookie consent, ToS
  Keep resolution at XGA (1024x768) or WXGA (1280x800) for best accuracy
  Log every action for post-incident forensic

Current limitations (Apr 2026):
  Latency too slow for real-time interactive work
  OSWorld benchmark: Anthropic 22 percent vs human 72 percent
  Account creation and social platform impersonation deliberately constrained
  Niche app reliability lower than mainstream app reliability

COMPARISON WITH OPENAI CUA AND WITH WKAPPBOT

Anthropic Computer Use vs OpenAI Computer-Using Agent (CUA):

  Dimension              Anthropic CU            OpenAI CUA
  Focus                  Desktop-first           Browser-first
  Environment            You provide             OpenAI cloud VM
  OSWorld                22 percent              38.1 percent
  WebVoyager             56 percent              87 percent
  Pricing                Pay-per-use API         200 USD/mo ChatGPT Pro
  Availability           Global                  US only
  Strength               Any desktop app         Web automation

Anthropic Computer Use vs WKAppBot approach:

  Dimension              Anthropic CU            WKAppBot
  Perception             Screenshot pixels       UIA tree + Win32 + CDP + OCR fallback
  Action                 Coords at scaled res    Semantic grap address plus focusless UIA
  Focus steal            Cursor moves visibly    Focusless-first (UIA Invoke, PostMessage)
  Reliability            Vision model hit-miss   Deterministic when UIA available
  Fallback when UIA N/A  Already screenshot      CCA + OCR + Vision API (Experience DB cache)
  Cost per action        Full screenshot + model Near-zero for UIA, OCR only when needed
  Cross-platform         Linux/Mac/Windows       Windows + Android (ADB)
  Use case               General desktop tasks   Legacy MFC/HTS + web + Android apps

Complementary use: WKAppBot for deterministic high-volume app automation,
Computer Use for general figure-it-out style unfamiliar app tasks.
Both give AI eyes and hands just at different abstraction layers.
author: claude
